item.title: OpenAI to nearly double workforce to 8,000 by end-2026, FT reports
item.description: Artificial intelligence start-up ​OpenAI plans ‌to nearly double ​its ​workforce to 8,000 from ⁠4,500 ​by the ​end of 2026, the Financial ​Times ​reported on Saturday, ‌citing ⁠two people with knowledge of ​the ​matter.

item.title: Iran war's energy impact forces world to pay up, cut consumption
item.description: The war in the Middle East has triggered a nightmare scenario for the global energy system, slashing so much supply that consumers around the world must both pay up big and lower consumption.

item.title: Mexico's Femsa cuts workers at Spin fintech division
item.description: Mexican retailer and bottler Femsa said on Friday it is laying ​off workers in its fintech unit Spin, which launched ‌a digital wallet app for payments and financial transactions in 2021.

item.title: United Airlines to cut 5% of scheduled flights as fuel prices soar
item.description: United Airlines CEO Scott ​Kirby said on Friday the airline ‌will cancel about 5% of planned flights in the short term, as jet fuel prices surge due to ​the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Exclusive: Pentagon to adopt Palantir AI as core US military system, memo says
item.description: Palantir’s Maven artificial intelligence system will become an official program of record, Deputy Secretary of Defense Steve ​Feinberg said in a letter to Pentagon leaders, a move that locks in long-term use of Palantir’s weapons-targeting technology across ‌the U.S. military.

item.title: Blackstone's flagship private credit fund posts first monthly loss in over three years
item.description: Blackstone's flagship private credit fund posted its first monthly loss in more than three years in February, the fund's website showed on Friday, amid surging investor ​worries over the sector's liquidity strains.

item.title: Musk found liable to Twitter shareholders in fraud lawsuit over $44 billion takeover, Bloomberg says
item.description: A federal jury found Elon Musk liable on claims he defrauded Twitter shareholders by trying to drive down the social media ​company's stock price so he could renegotiate or back out of a $44 billion takeover in ‌2022, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

item.title: Jury says Musk misled Twitter investors before 2022 buyout, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: A jury has concluded Elon Musk ​defrauded Twitter investors when ‌he disparaged the company in 2022 in ​an effort to ​buy the social media platform ⁠for a lower ​price than his original $44 ​billion bid, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

item.title: Sezzle dismisses auditor Baker Tilly, appoints PwC for 2026
item.description: Sezzle has dismissed its independent auditor Baker Tilly and ​instead appointed PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) for 2026, ‌the buy now, pay later company said in a filing on ​Monday.

item.title: Nuclear reactor developer X‑energy files for US IPO
item.description: X-energy ​has filed for ‌an initial public offering in ​the United States, ​it said on ⁠Friday, as the ​reactor developer aims ​to tap growing investor enthusiasm for nuclear ​power.

item.title: EyePoint sues rival Ocular Therapeutix, alleging false claims about eye drug
item.description: EyePoint filed a lawsuit on Friday accusing rival Ocular Therapeutix of spreading ​false or misleading information about EyePoint and its ‌lead experimental eye drug.

item.title: Super Micro co-founder Liaw resigns from board after US charge for smuggling AI chips to China
item.description: Super Micro ​Computer said on Friday that ‌Yih-Shyan Liaw has resigned from its board, effective immediately, after ​the co-founder was charged ​with helping smuggle billions of ⁠dollars of AI chips ​to China.

item.title: US appeals court tosses FTC order against Intuit over TurboTax advertising
item.description: A U.S. appeals court ​on Friday ‌threw out a Federal Trade ​Commission order ​barring Intuit from ⁠deceptively advertising ​its popular TurboTax ​products as "free" for simple tax returns.

item.title: Iran-linked hackers restore website after US seizes domains
item.description: The website used by ​an Iranian government-linked hacking unit that claimed responsibility for a March 11 cyberattack on ‌a U.S. medical device maker is back up and running a day after the FBI and Department of Justice seized its internet domains.

item.title: Roblox to overhaul ad policies, introduce revenue sharing in 2027
item.description: Videogame platform Roblox said on Friday it would take a portion of revenue from in-game brand deals starting next year, as part ​of a broader advertising-policy overhaul designed to draw in more ‌brand dollars and increase creator earnings.

item.title: Cyber actors linked to Russia are targeting users of commercial messaging apps, FBI says
item.description: The FBI ​has identified cyber ‌actors associated with Russian ​intelligence ​services targeting users of ⁠commercial ​messaging applications, including ​Signal, FBI Director Kash Patel ​said in ​a post on ‌X ⁠on Friday.

item.title: Judge for now dismisses lawsuit by Sam Altman's sister accusing OpenAI CEO of sexual abuse
item.description: A federal judge on Friday dismissed a lawsuit by Sam Altman's sister accusing ‌the billionaire OpenAI chief executive of sexually abusing her more than two decades ago, but said she can try suing under a different legal theory.

item.title: Suncor's global head of market and trade risk management to depart, sources say
item.description: The global head of market and trade risk management at Canadian oil producer Suncor ​Energy is set to leave the ​company, two sources said on Friday.

item.title: Eight states ask US judge to temporarily block Nexstar, Tegna merger
item.description: Eight states asked a U.S. judge on ​Friday to issue a ‌temporary restraining order to stop the $3.5 billion merger of ​Nexstar Media Group ​and Tegna.

item.title: Global bond rout deepens with concern over war-driven inflation
item.description: Government bond yields in the U.S. and Europe spiked on Friday as investor concern intensified over the inflationary impact of the war-driven global energy shock, with expectations the pressure will not ease ​anytime soon.

item.title: Nevada judge temporarily blocks Kalshi from operating prediction market in state
item.description: A ​Nevada judge on Friday temporarily blocked prediction market operator Kalshi from offering events contracts that ‌would allow the state's residents to place financial bets on its platform related to sports, elections and entertainment.

item.title: Wall St Week Ahead: Persistent Iran war, energy price surge set to loom over stocks
item.description: A Middle East crisis that has convulsed markets should remain the focal point for Wall Street in the near term, as investors stay glued to developments in Iran and the fallout from surging energy ​prices.

item.title: AI's demand for data could cause tight storage chip supplies, Solidigm executive says
item.description: The same booming AI demand that has caused the chairman of South Korea's SK Group to ​predict shortages of high-bandwidth memory chips could also cause tight ‌supplies for storage drives, an executive from the South Korean firm's U.S.-based AI subsidiary Solidigm told Reuters this week.

item.title: Markets bet on Fed rate hike as soon as July
item.description: Market pricing for a U.S. Federal Reserve interest-rate hike by September is about 75%, with better-than-even odds of ​a Fed rate hike as early as July.

item.title: Easter egg prices to stay high despite cocoa price crash, report says
item.description: Prices for chocolate ‌eggs and bunnies will remain high this Easter in the United States despite the fact that prices for the main raw material used to make ​them - cocoa beans - have gone sharply down over the ​last year, a report said on Friday.

item.title: Chubb announces war-risk coverage to support ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Insurance giant Chubb said on Friday its maritime insurance facility will now be available to ships transiting the Strait of ​Hormuz where traffic has been paralyzed due to the ‌U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran.

item.title: Senior housing REIT Janus Living valued at $5.9 billion as shares rise in NYSE debut
item.description: Janus Living's shares rose 17.5% in their New York Stock Exchange ​debut on Friday, giving the ‌U.S. seniors-focused real estate investment trust a valuation of $5.92 billion and marking a notable green ​shoot for the sector.

item.title: Brazil's new finance minister to delay divisive tax plans ahead of election, sources say
item.description: Brazil's new Finance Minister, Dario Durigan, plans to adjust the ministry's communication strategy and postpone ​tax measures, including rules on crypto taxation, as the ‌country heads into a presidential election this year, two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

item.title: From Frank's to Cholula, McCormick's decade of deals sets stage for bold Unilever move
item.description: Spice maker McCormick has built a reputation as a savvy acquirer in ‌the past decade, turning brands like Frank's RedHot and French's mustard into growth drivers that now account for a sizeable chunk of its $6.8 billion in annual sales, analysts say.

item.title: Exclusive: Qatar's energy boss says he had warned of dangers of provoking Iran
item.description: As Qatar reels from an Iranian attack that has hobbled its giant natural gas company, its boss, who doubles as the country's energy minister, ​says he had warned officials and executives of just such a danger should Iran's own sites be hit.
